content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges:
[(3, 7, 1), (0, 4, 4), (1, 6, 5), (7, 9, 7), (2, 5, 8), (1, 3, 11), (0, 6, 13), (2, 7, 18), (0, 1, 19), (0, 9, 22), (1, 7, 26), (3, 8, 26), (3, 9, 27), (3, 5, 28)],

We need to find the nodes linked with node 1 exactly at time 19.

First, we will scan each edge (u, v, t) and collect the other node whenever the time t matches the queried time AND one of the nodes matches the queried node:

* Edge (3, 7, 1) has node 3 and time 1. -> Add node 7.
* Edge (0, 4, 4) has node 0 and time 4. -> Add node 4.
* Edge (1, 6, 5) has node 1 and time 5. -> Add node 6.
* Edge (7, 9, 7) has node 7 and time 7. -> Add node 9.
* Edge (2, 5, 8) has node 2 and time 8. -> Add node 5.
* Edge (1, 3, 11) has node 1 and time 11. -> Add node 3.
* Edge (0, 6, 13) has node 0 and time 13. -> Add node 6.
* Edge (2, 7, 18) has node 2 and time 18. -> Add node 7.
* Edge (0, 1, 19) has node 0 and time 19. -> Add node 1.
* Edge (0, 9, 22) has node 0 and time 22. -> Add node 9.
* Edge (1, 7, 26) has node 1 and time 26. -> Add node 7.
* Edge (3, 8, 26) has node 3 and time 26. -> Add node 8.
* Edge (3, 9, 27) has node 3 and time 27. -> Add node 9.
* Edge (3, 5, 28) has node 3 and time 28. -> Add node 5.

Now we have the following nodes linked with node 1 at time 19:

1, 3, 7, 9, 6, 0, 4, 2, 5, 8, 18, 26, 27, 28

Therefore, the answer is:

[1, 3, 7, 9, 6, 0, 4, 2, 5, 8, 18, 26, 27, 28]
